lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: *sigh* Not another one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Another what?

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: Another 'archaeologist'.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: I'm not going to let you plunder my Master's tomb you know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a shame, have you got anything else I could do while I'm here?

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: If it will keep you out of mischief I suppose I could set something up for you...

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: I have a few rooms full of some things you humans might consider valuable, do you want to give it a go?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do?

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: You have five minutes to explore the treasure rooms and collect as many artefacts as you can.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: The artefacts are in the urns, chests and sarcophagi found in each room.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: There are eight treasure rooms, each subsequent room requires higher thieving skills to both enter the room and thieve from the urns and other containers.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: The rewards also become more lucrative the further into the tomb you go.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: You will also have to deactivate a trap in order to enter the main part of each room.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: When you want to move onto the next room you need to find the correct door first.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: There are four possible exits, you must open the door before finding out whether it is the exit or not.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: Opening the doors require picking their locks. Having a lockpick will make this easier.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I leave the game?

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: If at any point you decide you need to leave just use a glowing door.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: The game will end and you will be taken out of the pyramid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What about the chests and sarcophagi?

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: The artefacts are in the urns, chests and sarcophagi.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: Urns contain snakes that guard them.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: The sarcophagi take some strength to open. They take a while to open.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: Of course, Mummies have been known to take a nap in the sarcophagi, so beware.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: The golden chests generally contain better artefacts, but are also trapped with scarabs!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about these artefacts.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: There are a number of different artefacts, of three main types. The least valuable are the pottery statuettes and scarabs, and the ivory combs.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: Next are the stone scarabs, statuettes and seals, and finally the gold versions of those artefacts.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: They are not old, but are well made.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I do with artefacts once I've collected them?

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: That Simon Simpleton, I mean Templeton, will probably give you some money for them.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: He couldn't spot a real artefact if it came up to him and bit him in the face.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: He usually slinks about near the pyramid north-east of Sophanem. I expect he's trying to get some poor fools to steal things from that pyramid as well.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: I expect he'll give you more gold for some than others.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's this I've heard about a Golden Sceptre?

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: Where did you hear about that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I couldn't possibly say.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: It's the only genuinely valuable artefact in this place.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: It links all the great pyramids in the area, and can be used to travel between them.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: It requires charging with offerings of fine craftsmanship, that's why we have so many spare artefacts lying around.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: Anyway, I won't let you get your grubby little hands on the sceptre.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I get the artefacts?

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: The artefacts are in the urns, chests and sarcophagi.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: Urns contain snakes that guard them.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: The sarcophagi take some strength to open. They take a while to open.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: Of course, Mummies have been known to take a nap in the sarcophagi, so beware.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: The golden chests generally contain better artefacts, but are also trapped with scarabs!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I do with the artefacts I collect?

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: There are a number of different artefacts, of three main types. The least valuable are the pottery statuettes and scarabs, and the ivory combs.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: Next are the stone scarabs, statuettes and seals, and finally the gold versions of those artefacts.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: They are not old, but are well made.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I do with artefacts once I've collected them?

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: That Simon Simpleton, I mean Templeton, will probably give you some money for them.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: He couldn't spot a real artefact if it came up to him and bit him in the face.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: He usually slinks about near the pyramid north-east of Sophanem. I expect he's trying to get some poor fools to steal things from that pyramid as well.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: I expect he'll give you more gold for some than others.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not right now.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: Well, get out of here then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know what I'm doing - let's get on with it.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: Fine, I'll take you to the first room now...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to charge my sceptre.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: What sceptre?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... I don't know.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: Right...

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: You shouldn't have that thing in the first place, thief!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... If I give you back some of the artefacts I've taken from the tomb, will you recharge the sceptre for me?

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: No! Why would I waste the King's magic when it's already fully charged?

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: *sigh* Oh alright, but this is such a waste of the King's magic...

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: No! Why would I waste the King's magic when it's already fully charged?

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: *sigh* Oh alright, but this is such a waste of the King's magic...

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: You need to have 6 gold artefacts to recharge your sceptre.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: Right, you already had [x] charge[s], and I don't give discounts. That means 6 artefacts gives you [y] charge[s]. Now be on your way.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: Right, 6 artefacts gives you [x] charges. Now be on your way.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: You need to have 12 stone artefacts to recharge your sceptre.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: Right, you already had [x] charge[s], and I don't give discounts. That means 12 artefacts gives you [y] charge[s]. Now be on your way.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: Right, 12 artefacts gives you [x] charges. Now be on your way.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: You need to have 24 pottery or ivory artefacts to recharge your sceptre.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: Right, you already had [x] charge[s], and I don't give discounts. That means 24 artefacts gives you [y] charge[s]. Now be on your way.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: Right, 24 artefacts gives you [x] charges. Now be on your way.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: You've had your five minutes of plundering. Now be off with you!

sections.title: Pharaoh's sceptre or any Plundered artefacts while having a Pharaoh’s sceptre equipped or in their inventory

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to charge my sceptre.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: You shouldn't have that thing in the first place, thief!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... If I give you back some of the artefacts I've taken from the tomb, will you recharge the sceptre for me?

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: No! Why would I waste the King's magic when it's already fully charged?

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: *sigh* Oh alright, but this is such a waste of the King's magic...

sections.title: After charging a sceptre for the first time

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to charge my sceptre.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: No! Why would I waste the King's magic when it's already fully charged?

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: *sigh* Oh alright, but this is such a waste of the King's magic...

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: You need to have 6 gold artefacts to recharge your sceptre.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: Right, you already had [amount] charge[s], and I don't give discounts. That means 6 artefacts gives you [amount] charge[s]. Now be on your way.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: Right, 6 artefacts gives you [amount] charges. Now be on your way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I've changed my mind.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: Then clear off!

sections.title: Plundered artefacts without having a Pharaoh’s sceptre equipped or in their inventory

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you remind me what I am supposed to do with these?

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: That Simon Simpleton, I mean Templeton, will probably give you some money for them.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: He couldn't spot a real artefact if it came up to him and bit him in the face.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: I expect he'll give you more gold for some than others.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you!

sections.title: Obtaining a Pharaoh's sceptre from Pyramid Plunder

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: You have found my Master's Sceptre - You have been here long enough. Begone.

sections.title: Another player obtaining a Pharaoh's sceptre from Pyramid Plunder

lines.speaker: Annoyed guardian mummy
lines.text: Grrr! Someone has found the Pharaoh's sceptre!

lines.speaker: Annoyed guardian mummy
lines.text: Right! That's it. I'm clearing all the urns and refilling them now.

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: Scarabs can do puzzles better than that!

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: Excellent work!

lines.speaker: Guardian mummy
lines.text: You're not done yet! Do this for me!
